context: Pilates-integrated session covering centre practice, adage, pirouette enchaînement, Variation 2 on pointe

corrections.text: Pirouettes closing in 3rd not 5th — axis collapses before rotation finishes, think length up on the way down
Shoulders creeping up when arms go up — press down as opposition to the arm rising
Pull up before stepping onto pointe — passive foot on entry leads to instability
Lame duck preparation: small fourth, not fifth under the body before turning
Rotation comes from pulling the knee back, not from swinging the arm
Generate turn from the hip rotators, not the shoulder
Second shoulder hiking up in arabesque — outer hip and side body not stabilising the torso
Pull up more on the standing leg for stability in arabesque
Don't look down toward the floor in arabesque — eyeline up and over the arm
Hold turnout longer before the leg extends to arabesque in the fouetté — deep hip rotators releasing too early
Arms need to keep moving for fluidity through adage
Get onto the block faster on all poses in Variation 2 — outer ankle and foot not firing fast enough before weight transfer
Controlled rolling down from pointe — don't drop through the demi-pointe
Press into a deeper fondu before snapping into relevé — limited ankle bend restricting preparation depth

homeExercises.name: Side planks, all fours, supine twist
homeExercises.notes: Targeting obliques and side body for pirouette stability
